summary: Solomon consecrates his reign at Gibeon with a thousand offerings and humbly asks God for wisdom to lead Israel, receiving wisdom, wealth, and honor beyond all kings; Psalm 72 envisions the ideal righteous king whose dominion extends to the ends of the earth — a Messianic vision fulfilled in Christ.
psalm_rows_html:
PsalmTypeAuthorSituationKey ThemeNotable VerseNT Connection
72Royal / MessianicSolomon (or for Solomon, by David)Coronation prayer for the kingThe righteous king's universal, eternal reign of justice and peace for the poor"He shall have dominion also from sea to sea, and from the River to the ends of the earth." (v. 8)The Magi's gifts echo Ps 72:10–11; Rev 11:15 echoes the universal kingdom; Christ fulfills the ideal king this psalm envisions
